name: Mongolian Beef
description: Tender slices of flank steak coated in a savory-sweet soy and brown sugar sauce with garlic and ginger, served over steamed rice.
instructions: Toss thinly sliced flank steak with cornstarch until evenly coated. Set aside for 10 minutes.
In a small bowl, whisk together soy sauce, brown sugar, and water to make the sauce.
Heat 2 tablespoons of vegetable oil in a large wok or skillet over high heat until smoking.
Add the coated beef in a single layer and cook for 1-2 minutes per side until browned and crispy. Work in batches if needed. Remove beef and set aside.
Add remaining 1 tablespoon of oil to the pan. Sauté garlic and ginger for 30 seconds until fragrant.
Pour in the sauce mixture and stir. Bring to a boil and let it simmer for 2-3 minutes until slightly thickened.
Return the beef to the pan along with the green onions. Toss to coat everything in the sauce.
Cook for another 1-2 minutes until the sauce clings to the beef.
Garnish with sesame seeds and serve immediately over steamed white rice.
notes: For extra crispiness, shake off excess cornstarch before frying. The sauce can be made ahead and refrigerated for up to a week.

name: Onion Braised Brisket
description: A classic Jewish-style brisket slow-braised with caramelized onions, tomatoes, and red wine until fall-apart tender.
instructions: Preheat oven to 325°F.
Pat brisket dry and season all over with salt, pepper, and garlic powder.
Heat oil in a large Dutch oven over medium-high heat. Sear brisket fat-side down for 5-6 minutes until deep brown, then flip and sear the other side for 4-5 minutes. Remove and set aside.
Reduce heat to medium. Add onions to the same pot and cook, stirring occasionally, for 20-25 minutes until deeply caramelized.
Add garlic and tomato paste, cook for 1-2 minutes.
Pour in red wine and scrape up any browned bits from the bottom of the pot. Cook for 2 minutes.
Add diced tomatoes and beef broth. Stir to combine.
Return brisket to the pot, fat-side up. Add thyme and bay leaves. The liquid should come about halfway up the brisket.
Cover tightly with a lid and braise in the oven for 3 to 3.5 hours, until fork-tender.
Remove brisket and let rest for 15 minutes before slicing against the grain.
Skim fat from the braising liquid and serve as sauce. Garnish with fresh parsley.
notes: This dish is even better the next day. Refrigerate overnight, remove solidified fat, then slice and reheat in the braising liquid.

name: Classic Pot Roast
description: A hearty one-pot meal with tender chuck roast slow-cooked with potatoes, carrots, and celery in a rich herb-infused broth.
instructions: Preheat oven to 300°F.
Pat the chuck roast dry and season generously on all sides with salt and pepper.
Heat olive oil in a large Dutch oven over medium-high heat. Sear roast for 4-5 minutes per side until a deep brown crust forms. Remove and set aside.
Add onion to the pot and cook for 3-4 minutes. Add garlic and tomato paste, cook for 1 minute more.
Pour in beef broth and Worcestershire sauce, scraping up any browned bits.
Return the roast to the pot. Tuck in rosemary, thyme, and bay leaves.
Cover and cook in the oven for 2 hours.
Add potatoes, carrots, and celery around the roast. Cover and continue cooking for another 1.5 to 2 hours until the beef is fork-tender and vegetables are soft.
Remove herbs and bay leaves. Taste broth and adjust seasoning.
Serve the roast sliced or pulled apart, with vegetables and broth spooned over the top.
notes: For a thicker gravy, remove the roast and vegetables after cooking, then simmer the liquid on the stovetop with a slurry of 2 tbsp cornstarch and 2 tbsp cold water.

name: Ground Beef Tacos
description: Perfectly seasoned ground beef in crispy taco shells loaded with fresh toppings — a quick and satisfying weeknight favorite.
instructions: In a large skillet over medium-high heat, cook ground beef and onion, breaking up the meat, until no pink remains, about 7-8 minutes. Drain excess fat.
Add garlic and cook for 30 seconds.
Stir in chili powder, cumin, smoked paprika, garlic powder, onion powder, and salt.
Pour in broth or water. Stir to combine and simmer for 2-3 minutes until the liquid is absorbed and the mixture is well-seasoned.
Warm taco shells according to package directions.
Fill each taco shell with seasoned beef, then top with shredded cheese, lettuce, diced tomatoes, sour cream, and salsa.
Serve immediately.
notes: Substitute corn tortillas for a softer taco. Add sliced jalapeños or guacamole for extra toppings.

name: Crockpot Stuffed Peppers
description: Colorful bell peppers stuffed with a savory mixture of ground beef, rice, and tomato sauce, slow-cooked until tender and topped with melted cheese.

ingredients.name: large bell peppers
ingredients.prepNote: tops cut off and seeds removed

instructions: In a skillet over medium-high heat, brown ground beef with onion until cooked through. Drain fat.
Add garlic and cook for 30 seconds. Remove from heat.
In a large bowl, combine cooked beef mixture, uncooked rice, half of the tomato sauce, diced tomatoes, Italian seasoning, salt, pepper, and Worcestershire sauce. Mix well.
Spoon the filling into each bell pepper, pressing gently to pack.
Pour the remaining tomato sauce and beef broth into the bottom of the slow cooker.
Stand the stuffed peppers upright in the slow cooker.
Cover and cook on LOW for 4-5 hours or HIGH for 2.5-3 hours, until the peppers are tender and the rice is cooked through.
Top each pepper with shredded mozzarella, cover for 5 minutes to melt.
Garnish with fresh parsley and serve.
notes: Use a mix of red, yellow, and green bell peppers for a colorful presentation. For firmer rice, use parboiled rice.

name: Sloppy Joes
description: Classic American comfort food with seasoned ground beef simmered in a tangy-sweet tomato sauce, piled high on toasted hamburger buns.
instructions: In a large skillet over medium-high heat, cook ground beef, onion, and green bell pepper until the beef is browned and vegetables are softened, about 8-10 minutes. Drain excess fat.
Add garlic and cook for 1 minute.
Stir in tomato sauce, ketchup, brown sugar, mustard, Worcestershire sauce, apple cider vinegar, chili powder, salt, and pepper.
Reduce heat to medium-low and simmer, stirring occasionally, for 15 minutes until the sauce thickens.
Taste and adjust seasoning as needed.
Toast hamburger buns if desired.
Spoon the sloppy joe mixture generously onto the bottom buns and top with the other half. Serve immediately.
notes: Leftovers keep well in the fridge for up to 4 days and taste even better the next day. The mixture also freezes well for up to 3 months.

name: Steak Fajitas
description: Sizzling marinated skirt steak with colorful bell peppers and onions, served with warm flour tortillas and all the classic fixings.
instructions: In a bowl or zip-lock bag, combine lime juice, 2 tablespoons olive oil, garlic, chili powder, cumin, smoked paprika, salt, and pepper.
Add skirt steak to the marinade, turning to coat. Marinate for at least 30 minutes (or up to 4 hours in the refrigerator).
Remove steak from marinade and pat dry.
Heat a large cast-iron skillet or grill pan over high heat until very hot. Add 1 tablespoon olive oil.
Cook steak for 3-4 minutes per side for medium-rare, or to your preferred doneness. Let rest for 5 minutes, then slice against the grain into thin strips.
In the same skillet, add bell peppers and onion. Cook over high heat, stirring occasionally, for 5-7 minutes until charred and tender.
Warm flour tortillas in a dry skillet or wrapped in damp paper towels in the microwave.
Serve steak and peppers on warm tortillas with sour cream, guacamole, salsa, and cheese.
notes: Skirt steak can be substituted with flank steak or flat iron steak. Slicing against the grain is essential for tenderness.

name: Pork Tenderloin
description: A juicy, herb-crusted pork tenderloin seared to a golden crust and roasted to perfection, ready in under 40 minutes.
instructions: Preheat oven to 425°F.
Pat pork tenderloin dry. Remove any silver skin with a sharp knife.
In a small bowl, combine olive oil, garlic, rosemary, thyme, Dijon mustard, salt, pepper, garlic powder, and smoked paprika.
Rub the herb mixture all over the tenderloin.
Heat an oven-safe skillet over medium-high heat. Add butter and sear the pork on all sides, about 2 minutes per side, until golden brown.
Transfer the skillet to the preheated oven. Roast for 15-18 minutes until an internal thermometer reads 145°F.
Remove from oven and tent loosely with foil. Rest for 5-10 minutes before slicing.
Slice into medallions and serve with pan drippings spooned over the top.
notes: Do not overcook pork tenderloin — it dries out quickly. A meat thermometer is highly recommended. An internal temperature of 145°F with a 3-minute rest is safe and results in a slightly pink, very juicy center.

name: Pulled Pork
description: Melt-in-your-mouth slow-cooked pork shoulder rubbed with a smoky spice blend and finished in tangy barbecue sauce — perfect for sandwiches or plates.
instructions: In a small bowl, combine brown sugar, smoked paprika, garlic powder, onion powder, chili powder, cumin, salt, pepper, and cayenne to make the dry rub.
Pat pork shoulder dry and coat all over with the dry rub, pressing it in firmly. For best results, refrigerate uncovered overnight.
Place apple cider vinegar and chicken broth in the bottom of a slow cooker.
Place pork shoulder in the slow cooker, fat-side up.
Cook on LOW for 8-10 hours or HIGH for 5-6 hours until the meat is fall-apart tender.
Remove pork from the slow cooker and place on a large cutting board or sheet pan.
Using two forks, shred the meat, discarding any large pieces of fat or bone.
Return shredded pork to the slow cooker and mix with cooking juices and BBQ sauce.
Cook on HIGH for an additional 15-20 minutes to let the sauce soak in.
Serve on toasted buns with additional BBQ sauce and coleslaw.
notes: For a crispy bark reminiscent of smoked pulled pork, spread the shredded meat on a baking sheet and broil for 3-5 minutes after shredding.

name: Baby Back Ribs
description: Fall-off-the-bone tender baby back ribs coated in a smoky dry rub, oven-baked low and slow, then slathered with BBQ sauce and caramelized to perfection.
instructions: Preheat oven to 275°F.
Remove the membrane from the back of the ribs by sliding a knife under it and peeling it off.
Combine brown sugar, smoked paprika, garlic powder, onion powder, chili powder, cumin, dry mustard, salt, pepper, and cayenne in a bowl.
Rub the spice mixture all over both sides of the ribs, pressing firmly.
Place ribs bone-side down on a large sheet of heavy-duty aluminum foil. Pour apple juice around the ribs.
Wrap the ribs tightly in the foil and place on a baking sheet.
Bake for 2.5 to 3 hours until the ribs are very tender and the meat has pulled back from the bones.
Carefully open the foil (watch for steam). Increase oven to 425°F or turn on the broiler.
Brush both sides of the ribs generously with BBQ sauce.
Return to oven uncovered and bake or broil for 10-15 minutes until the sauce is caramelized and sticky.
Let rest for 5 minutes, then cut into individual ribs and serve with extra BBQ sauce.
notes: For extra tender ribs, you can bake them the day before through step 7, refrigerate, then finish with the BBQ sauce glaze just before serving.

name: Ground Beef Stir Fry
description: A fast and flavorful stir fry with seasoned ground beef, crisp vegetables, and a savory garlic-ginger sauce served over fluffy rice.
instructions: In a small bowl, whisk together soy sauce, oyster sauce, sesame oil, brown sugar, and cornstarch. Set aside.
Heat 1 tablespoon vegetable oil in a large wok or skillet over high heat.
Add ground beef and cook, breaking it up, until browned and cooked through. Drain excess fat. Remove and set aside.
Add remaining tablespoon of oil to the wok. Add garlic and ginger, stir-fry for 30 seconds.
Add carrots and broccoli. Stir-fry for 2-3 minutes until slightly tender.
Add bell pepper and snap peas. Cook for another 2 minutes.
Return ground beef to the wok. Pour the sauce over everything.
Toss to coat and stir-fry for 1-2 minutes until sauce thickens and everything is heated through.
Add red pepper flakes and toss.
Garnish with sliced green onions and sesame seeds. Serve over steamed white rice.
notes: Feel free to swap in any vegetables you have on hand. Frozen vegetables work well — just add them straight from the freezer and cook an extra minute or two.

name: Easy Crockpot Pepper Steak
description: Tender strips of beef slowly braised with bell peppers, onions, and a savory tomato-soy sauce in the slow cooker — effortless comfort food over rice.
instructions: Place sliced beef strips in the bottom of the slow cooker.
Add bell peppers, onion, and garlic on top of the beef.
In a bowl, whisk together diced tomatoes, beef broth, soy sauce, Worcestershire sauce, brown sugar, garlic powder, onion powder, and black pepper.
Pour the sauce mixture over the beef and vegetables.
Cover and cook on LOW for 6-8 hours or HIGH for 3-4 hours, until the beef is very tender.
About 15 minutes before serving, mix cornstarch and cold water together in a small bowl to make a slurry.
Stir the slurry into the slow cooker. Increase heat to HIGH, cover, and cook for an additional 15 minutes until the sauce thickens.
Taste and adjust seasoning if needed.
Serve over steamed white rice, garnished with sliced green onions.
notes: Flank steak or round steak also work well in this recipe. Partially freezing the beef for 20 minutes before cutting makes it much easier to slice thinly.
